78: function buildDeploymentIdempotencyPayload(requestPayload: {
79:   provider: string;
80:   retry: { maxRetries: number };
81:   validation: { runBuildIfPresent: boolean; runTestsIfPresent: boolean };
82:   autoFix: { rehydrateBaseline: boolean; bootstrapToolchain: boolean };
83:   toolchain: { manager: string | null; version: string | null };
84:   cache: { dependencyCache: boolean };
85:   deploy: { outputDir: string | null };
86:   rollbackOnFailure: boolean;
87:   provenance: { trigger: string; taskId: string | null; operationId: string | null; note: string | null };
88: }): Record<string, unknown> {
89:   const payload: Record<string, unknown> = {
90:     provider: requestPayload.provider,
91:     retry: requestPayload.retry,
92:     validation: requestPayload.validation,
93:     rollbackOnFailure: requestPayload.rollbackOnFailure,
94:     provenance: requestPayload.provenance,
95:   };
96: 
97:   if (requestPayload.autoFix.rehydrateBaseline || requestPayload.autoFix.bootstrapToolchain) {
98:     payload.autoFix = requestPayload.autoFix;
99:   }
100: 
101:   if (requestPayload.toolchain.manager || requestPayload.toolchain.version) {
102:     payload.toolchain = requestPayload.toolchain;
103:   }
104: 
105:   if (!requestPayload.cache.dependencyCache) {
106:     payload.cache = requestPayload.cache;
107:   }
108: 
109:   if (requestPayload.deploy.outputDir) {
110:     payload.deploy = requestPayload.deploy;
111:   }
112: 
113:   return payload;
114: }

116: function nextActionForDeploymentError(code: string | undefined): string | null {
117:   switch (code) {
118:     case 'toolchain_detect_failed':
119:       return 'Verify package.json and lockfile metadata, then retry deploy.';
120:     case 'corepack_missing':
121:       return 'Use a sandbox image with corepack available, or switch to npm toolchain.';
122:     case 'package_manager_bootstrap_failed':
123:       return 'Confirm the requested package manager version is valid and retry deploy.';
124:     case 'validation_tool_missing':
125:       return 'Disable build/test validation for this deploy or install required tooling in the sandbox image.';
126:     case 'validation_command_failed':
127:       return 'Review test/build output, fix project errors, and retry deploy.';
128:     case 'invalid_project_root':
129:       return 'Set workspace source project root to a safe relative path and retry deploy.';
130:     case 'baseline_missing':
131:     case 'baseline_rehydrate_failed':
132:       return 'Reset the workspace and retry deploy to rebuild git baseline.';
133:     case 'potential_secrets_detected':
134:       return 'Remove sensitive files from workspace before deploying (template files like .env.example are allowed).';
135:     case 'provider_auth_failed':
136:       return 'Verify CF_ACCOUNT_ID and CF_API_TOKEN, then rerun preflight.';
137:     case 'provider_scope_missing':
138:       return 'Grant Workers Scripts/Routes edit scope to CF_API_TOKEN and rerun preflight.';
139:     case 'provider_project_not_found':
140:       return 'Set WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PROJECT_NAME to an existing Workers project and retry.';
141:     case 'provider_rate_limited':
142:       return 'Retry deploy after Cloudflare rate limits reset.';
143:     case 'provider_invalid_output_dir':
144:       return 'Set deploy.outputDir to a valid static build directory (for example dist or out) and retry.';
145:     case 'provider_deploy_failed':
146:       return 'Check provider deployment logs and retry after fixing configuration or build output.';
147:     default:
148:       return null;
149:   }
150: }

152: async function sha256Hex(input: string): Promise<string> {
153:   const digest = await crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', new TextEncoder().encode(input));
154:   return Array.from(new Uint8Array(digest))
155:     .map((byte) => byte.toString(16).padStart(2, '0'))
156:     .join('');
157: }

203: export async function handleCreateWorkspaceDeployment(
204:   workspaceId: string,
205:   request: Request,
206:   env: Env,
207:   ctx?: ExecutionContext
208: ): Promise<Response> {
209:   try {
210:     const enabled = await ensureWorkspaceDeployEnabled(env);
211:     if (enabled) {
212:       return enabled;
213:     }
214: 
215:     const workspaceCheck = await ensureWorkspaceReady(env, workspaceId);
216:     if (workspaceCheck) {
217:       return workspaceCheck;
218:     }
219: 
220:     if (!env.WORKSPACE_DEPLOYS_QUEUE && !ctx) {
221:       return jsonResponse(
222:         {
223:           error: 'Workspace deployment runner is unavailable',
224:           code: 'workspace_deploy_runner_unavailable',
225:         },
226:         503
227:       );
228:     }
229: 
230:     const idempotencyKey = (request.headers.get('Idempotency-Key') ?? '').trim();
231:     if (!idempotencyKey) {
232:       return jsonResponse({ error: 'Missing required Idempotency-Key header' }, 400);
233:     }
234: 
235:     const payloadRaw = await request.text();
236:     let payload: Record<string, unknown> = {};
237:     if (payloadRaw.trim()) {
238:       const parsed = JSON.parse(payloadRaw) as unknown;
239:       if (!parsed || typeof parsed !== 'object' || Array.isArray(parsed)) {
240:         return jsonResponse({ error: 'Request body must be a JSON object' }, 400);
241:       }
242:       payload = parsed as Record<string, unknown>;
243:     }
244: 
245:     const providerInput = typeof payload.provider === 'string' ? payload.provider.trim() : '';
246:     if (providerInput && providerInput !== 'simulated' && providerInput !== 'cloudflare_workers_assets') {
247:       return jsonResponse(
248:         {
249:           error: 'Unsupported deployment provider',
250:           code: 'unsupported_deploy_provider',
251:           allowedProviders: ['simulated', 'cloudflare_workers_assets'],
252:         },
253:         400
254:       );
255:     }
256:     const provider = getWorkspaceDeployProviderName(providerInput || undefined, env);
257:     const retry =
258:       payload.retry && typeof payload.retry === 'object' && !Array.isArray(payload.retry)
259:         ? (payload.retry as Record<string, unknown>)
260:         : {};
261:     const validation =
262:       payload.validation && typeof payload.validation === 'object' && !Array.isArray(payload.validation)
263:         ? (payload.validation as Record<string, unknown>)
264:         : {};
265:     const provenance =
266:       payload.provenance && typeof payload.provenance === 'object' && !Array.isArray(payload.provenance)
267:         ? (payload.provenance as Record<string, unknown>)
268:         : {};
269:     const autoFix =
270:       payload.autoFix && typeof payload.autoFix === 'object' && !Array.isArray(payload.autoFix)
271:         ? (payload.autoFix as Record<string, unknown>)
272:         : {};
273:     const toolchain =
274:       payload.toolchain && typeof payload.toolchain === 'object' && !Array.isArray(payload.toolchain)
275:         ? (payload.toolchain as Record<string, unknown>)
276:         : {};
277:     const cache =
278:       payload.cache && typeof payload.cache === 'object' && !Array.isArray(payload.cache)
279:         ? (payload.cache as Record<string, unknown>)
280:         : {};
281:     const deploy =
282:       payload.deploy && typeof payload.deploy === 'object' && !Array.isArray(payload.deploy)
283:         ? (payload.deploy as Record<string, unknown>)
284:         : {};
285:     const maxRetries = parseInteger(retry.maxRetries, 2, 0, 5);
286:     const outputDir = parseDeployOutputDir(deploy.outputDir);
287: 
288:     if (provider === 'cloudflare_workers_assets' && (!outputDir || !isSafeRelativeOutputDir(outputDir))) {
289:       return jsonResponse(
290:         {
291:           error: 'deploy.outputDir is required for cloudflare_workers_assets provider and must be a safe relative directory',
292:           code: 'provider_invalid_output_dir',
293:         },
294:         400
295:       );
296:     }
297: 
298:     const requestPayload = {
299:       provider,
300:       retry: {
301:         maxRetries,
302:       },
303:       validation: {
304:         runBuildIfPresent: parseBoolean(validation.runBuildIfPresent, true),
305:         runTestsIfPresent: parseBoolean(validation.runTestsIfPresent, true),
306:       },
307:       autoFix: {
308:         rehydrateBaseline: parseBoolean(autoFix.rehydrateBaseline, false),
309:         bootstrapToolchain: parseBoolean(autoFix.bootstrapToolchain, false),
310:       },
311:       toolchain: {
312:         manager: typeof toolchain.manager === 'string' && toolchain.manager.trim() ? toolchain.manager.trim() : null,
313:         version: typeof toolchain.version === 'string' && toolchain.version.trim() ? toolchain.version.trim() : null,
314:       },
315:       cache: {
316:         dependencyCache: parseBoolean(cache.dependencyCache, true),
317:       },
318:       deploy: {
319:         outputDir,
320:       },
321:       rollbackOnFailure: parseBoolean(payload.rollbackOnFailure, true),
322:       provenance: {
323:         trigger: typeof provenance.trigger === 'string' && provenance.trigger.trim() ? provenance.trigger.trim() : 'manual',
324:         taskId: typeof provenance.taskId === 'string' && provenance.taskId.trim() ? provenance.taskId.trim() : null,
325:         operationId:
326:           typeof provenance.operationId === 'string' && provenance.operationId.trim() ? provenance.operationId.trim() : null,
327:         note: typeof provenance.note === 'string' && provenance.note.trim() ? provenance.note.trim() : null,
328:       },
329:     };
330: 
331:     const requestPayloadSha256 = await sha256Hex(
332:       JSON.stringify(buildDeploymentIdempotencyPayload(requestPayload))
333:     );
334: 
335:     const created = await createWorkspaceDeployment(env.DB, {
336:       id: generateWorkspaceDeploymentId(),
337:       workspaceId,
338:       provider,
339:       idempotencyKey,
340:       requestPayload,
341:       requestPayloadSha256,
342:       maxRetries,
343:       provenance: requestPayload.provenance,
344:     });
345: 
346:     if (!created.reused) {
347:       await appendWorkspaceDeploymentEvent(env.DB, {
348:         workspaceId,
349:         deploymentId: created.deployment.id,
350:         eventType: 'deployment_created',
351:         payload: {
352:           provider,
353:           maxRetries,
354:           provenance: requestPayload.provenance,
355:         },
356:       });
357:     }
358: 
359:     if (provider === 'cloudflare_workers_assets' && created.deployment.status === 'queued') {
360:       const hasPrecheckPassed = await hasWorkspaceDeploymentEvent(
361:         env.DB,
362:         workspaceId,
363:         created.deployment.id,
364:         'deployment_provider_precheck_passed'
365:       );
366:       const hasPrecheckFailed = await hasWorkspaceDeploymentEvent(
367:         env.DB,
368:         workspaceId,
369:         created.deployment.id,
370:         'deployment_provider_precheck_failed'
371:       );
372:       const alreadyEnqueued = await hasWorkspaceDeploymentEvent(
373:         env.DB,
374:         workspaceId,
375:         created.deployment.id,
376:         'deployment_enqueued'
377:       );
378:       const shouldRunPrecheck = !hasPrecheckPassed && !hasPrecheckFailed && !alreadyEnqueued;
379: 
380:       if (hasPrecheckFailed) {
381:         return jsonResponse(
382:           {
383:             error: created.deployment.error?.message ?? 'Provider precheck previously failed',
384:             code: created.deployment.error?.code ?? 'provider_deploy_failed',
385:           },
386:           400
387:         );
388:       }
389: 
390:       if (!shouldRunPrecheck) {
391:         // Precheck already passed/failed or deployment has already been enqueued.
392:       } else {
393:       try {
394:         const precheckChecks = await createWorkspaceDeployProvider(provider, env).precheck();
395:         const failed = precheckChecks.find((check) => !check.ok);
396:         if (failed) {
397:           await appendWorkspaceDeploymentEvent(env.DB, {
398:             workspaceId,
399:             deploymentId: created.deployment.id,
400:             eventType: 'deployment_provider_precheck_failed',
401:             payload: {
402:               code: failed.code,
403:               message: failed.details ?? 'Provider precheck failed',
404:             },
405:           });
406:           await updateWorkspaceDeploymentStatus(env.DB, created.deployment.id, 'failed', {
407:             workspaceId,
408:             errorCode: failed.code,
409:             errorMessage: failed.details ?? 'Provider precheck failed',
410:             finishedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
411:           });
412:           return jsonResponse(
413:             {
414:               error: failed.details ?? 'Provider precheck failed',
415:               code: failed.code,
416:             },
417:             400
418:           );
419:         }
420:         await appendWorkspaceDeploymentEvent(env.DB, {
421:           workspaceId,
422:           deploymentId: created.deployment.id,
423:           eventType: 'deployment_provider_precheck_passed',
424:           payload: {
425:             checks: precheckChecks,
426:           },
427:         });
428:       } catch (error) {
429:         const providerError = normalizeProviderError(error);
430:         await appendWorkspaceDeploymentEvent(env.DB, {
431:           workspaceId,
432:           deploymentId: created.deployment.id,
433:           eventType: 'deployment_provider_precheck_failed',
434:           payload: {
435:             code: providerError.code,
436:             message: providerError.message,
437:           },
438:         });
439:         await updateWorkspaceDeploymentStatus(env.DB, created.deployment.id, 'failed', {
440:           workspaceId,
441:           errorCode: providerError.code,
442:           errorMessage: providerError.message,
443:           finishedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
444:         });
445:         return jsonResponse({ error: providerError.message, code: providerError.code }, 400);
446:       }
447:       }
448:     }
449: 
450:     if (created.deployment.status === 'queued') {
451:       const hasEnqueuedEvent = await hasWorkspaceDeploymentEvent(
452:         env.DB,
453:         workspaceId,
454:         created.deployment.id,
455:         'deployment_enqueued'
456:       );
457:       const shouldRecoverQueued = created.reused && created.deployment.error?.code === 'retry_scheduled';
458:       const hasRecoveredReenqueue = shouldRecoverQueued
459:         ? await hasWorkspaceDeploymentEvent(env.DB, workspaceId, created.deployment.id, 'deployment_reenqueue_recovered')
460:         : false;
461: 
462:       if (!hasEnqueuedEvent || (shouldRecoverQueued && !hasRecoveredReenqueue)) {
463:         if (env.WORKSPACE_DEPLOYS_QUEUE) {
464:           await env.WORKSPACE_DEPLOYS_QUEUE.send(
465:             createWorkspaceDeploymentQueueMessage(workspaceId, created.deployment.id)
466:           );
467:         } else if (ctx) {
468:           ctx.waitUntil(
469:             runWorkspaceDeploymentInlineWithRetries(env, workspaceId, created.deployment.id, created.deployment.maxRetries + 1)
470:           );
471:         }
472: 
473:         await appendWorkspaceDeploymentEvent(env.DB, {
474:           workspaceId,
475:           deploymentId: created.deployment.id,
476:           eventType: 'deployment_enqueued',
477:           payload: {
478:             mode: env.WORKSPACE_DEPLOYS_QUEUE ? 'queue' : 'inline',
479:             reused: created.reused,
480:           },
481:         });
482: 
483:         if (shouldRecoverQueued && !hasRecoveredReenqueue) {
484:           await appendWorkspaceDeploymentEvent(env.DB, {
485:             workspaceId,
486:             deploymentId: created.deployment.id,
487:             eventType: 'deployment_reenqueue_recovered',
488:             payload: {
489:               reason: 'retry_scheduled_replay',
490:             },
491:           });
492:         }
493:       }
494:     }
495: 
496:     return jsonResponse({ deployment: created.deployment }, created.reused ? 200 : 202);
497:   } catch (error) {
498:     if (error instanceof WorkspaceDeploymentIdempotencyConflictError) {
499:       return jsonResponse(
500:         {
501:           error: 'Idempotency key has already been used with different payload',
502:           code: 'idempotency_key_conflict',
503:         },
504:         409
505:       );
506:     }
507: 
508:     if (error instanceof SyntaxError) {
509:       return jsonResponse({ error: 'Invalid JSON body' }, 400);
510:     }
511: 
512:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
513:     return jsonResponse({ error: `Failed to create workspace deployment: ${message}` }, 500);
514:   }
515: }

517: export async function handleWorkspaceDeploymentPreflight(
518:   workspaceId: string,
519:   request: Request,
520:   env: Env
521: ): Promise<Response> {
522:   const enabled = await ensureWorkspaceDeployEnabled(env);
523:   if (enabled) {
524:     return enabled;
525:   }
526: 
527:   const workspaceCheck = await ensureWorkspaceReady(env, workspaceId);
528:   if (workspaceCheck) {
529:     return workspaceCheck;
530:   }
531: 
532:   let payload: Record<string, unknown> = {};
533:   try {
534:     const raw = await request.text();
535:     if (raw.trim()) {
536:       const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as unknown;
537:       if (!parsed || typeof parsed !== 'object' || Array.isArray(parsed)) {
538:         return jsonResponse({ error: 'Request body must be a JSON object' }, 400);
539:       }
540:       payload = parsed as Record<string, unknown>;
541:     }
542:   } catch {
543:     return jsonResponse({ error: 'Invalid JSON body' }, 400);
544:   }
545: 
546:   const validation =
547:     payload.validation && typeof payload.validation === 'object' && !Array.isArray(payload.validation)
548:       ? (payload.validation as Record<string, unknown>)
549:       : {};
550:   const autoFix =
551:     payload.autoFix && typeof payload.autoFix === 'object' && !Array.isArray(payload.autoFix)
552:       ? (payload.autoFix as Record<string, unknown>)
553:       : {};
554:   const deploy =
555:     payload.deploy && typeof payload.deploy === 'object' && !Array.isArray(payload.deploy)
556:       ? (payload.deploy as Record<string, unknown>)
557:       : {};
558:   const providerInput = typeof payload.provider === 'string' ? payload.provider.trim() : '';
559:   if (providerInput && providerInput !== 'simulated' && providerInput !== 'cloudflare_workers_assets') {
560:     return jsonResponse(
561:       {
562:         preflight: {
563:           ok: false,
564:           toolchain: null,
565:           checks: [{ code: 'unsupported_deploy_provider', ok: false, details: `Unsupported provider: ${providerInput}` }],
566:           remediations: [],
567:         },
568:         nextAction: 'Use provider=cloudflare_workers_assets or provider=simulated.',
569:       },
570:       200
571:     );
572:   }
573:   const provider = getWorkspaceDeployProviderName(providerInput || undefined, env);
574:   const outputDir = parseDeployOutputDir(deploy.outputDir);
575: 
576:   if (provider === 'cloudflare_workers_assets' && (!outputDir || !isSafeRelativeOutputDir(outputDir))) {
577:     return jsonResponse(
578:       {
579:         preflight: {
580:           ok: false,
581:           toolchain: null,
582:           checks: [
583:             {
584:               code: 'provider_invalid_output_dir',
585:               ok: false,
586:               details: 'deploy.outputDir is required and must be a safe relative directory',
587:             },
588:           ],
589:           remediations: [],
590:         },
591:         nextAction: 'Set deploy.outputDir to your static build output directory and retry preflight.',
592:       },
593:       200
594:     );
595:   }
596:   const runBuildIfPresent = parseBoolean(validation.runBuildIfPresent, true);
597:   const runTestsIfPresent = parseBoolean(validation.runTestsIfPresent, true);
598:   const rehydrateBaseline = parseBoolean(autoFix.rehydrateBaseline, false);
599:   const bootstrapToolchain = parseBoolean(autoFix.bootstrapToolchain, false);
600: 
601:   try {
602:     const preflight = await runWorkspaceDeploymentPreflight(env, workspaceId, {
603:       runBuildIfPresent,
604:       runTestsIfPresent,
605:       rehydrateBaseline,
606:       bootstrapToolchain,
607:       provider,
608:       outputDir,
609:     });
610: 
611:     if (provider === 'cloudflare_workers_assets') {
612:       try {
613:         const providerChecks = await createWorkspaceDeployProvider(provider, env).precheck();
614:         preflight.checks.push(...providerChecks);
615:       } catch (error) {
616:         const providerError = normalizeProviderError(error);
617:         preflight.checks.push({ code: providerError.code, ok: false, details: providerError.message });
618:       }
619:       preflight.ok = preflight.checks.every((check) => check.ok);
620:     }
621: 
622:     const failedCheck = preflight.checks.find((check) => !check.ok);
623:     const nextAction = failedCheck
624:       ? failedCheck.code === 'validation_tooling'
625:         ? 'Disable build/test validation or install the detected package manager in the sandbox runtime image.'
626:         : failedCheck.code === 'git_baseline'
627:           ? 'Reset workspace to rebuild git baseline and retry deploy.'
628:           : failedCheck.code === 'secret_scan'
629:             ? 'Remove sensitive files from workspace before deploying.'
630:             : failedCheck.code === 'toolchain_detect'
631:               ? 'Fix package.json/lockfile metadata and retry preflight.'
632:               : failedCheck.code === 'toolchain_bootstrap'
633:                 ? 'Enable auto-fix bootstrap or use a sandbox image with corepack support.'
634:                 : failedCheck.code === 'project_root'
635:                   ? 'Set workspace source project root to a safe relative path and retry preflight.'
636:                   : failedCheck.code === 'provider_invalid_output_dir'
637:                     ? 'Set deploy.outputDir to a valid static build output directory and retry preflight.'
638:                     : failedCheck.code === 'provider_auth_failed'
639:                       ? 'Verify Cloudflare account credentials in worker env and retry preflight.'
640:                       : failedCheck.code === 'provider_scope_missing'
641:                         ? 'Grant required Cloudflare token scopes and retry preflight.'
642:                         : failedCheck.code === 'provider_project_not_found'
643:                           ? 'Set WORKSPACE_DEPLOY_PROJECT_NAME to a valid Workers project and retry preflight.'
644:                           : failedCheck.code === 'provider_rate_limited'
645:                             ? 'Wait for provider rate limits to reset and retry preflight.'
646:             : null
647:       : null;
648:     return jsonResponse({ preflight, nextAction });
649:   } catch (error) {
650:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
651:     return jsonResponse(
652:       {
653:         preflight: {
654:           ok: false,
655:           toolchain: null,
656:           checks: [{ code: 'internal_error', ok: false, details: message }],
657:           remediations: [],
658:         },
659:       },
660:       500
661:     );
662:   }
663: }

687:   workspaceId: string,
688:   deploymentId: string,

690:   env: Env
691: ): Promise<Response> {

697:   const deployment = await getWorkspaceDeployment(env.DB, workspaceId, deploymentId);

parts.text:
Found 1 blocking issue.

- **Race still allows enqueue after precheck failure** in `packages/worker/src/api/workspace-deployments.ts:359` and `packages/worker/src/api/workspace-deployments.ts:450`.  
  The handler decides `shouldRunPrecheck`/`hasEnqueuedEvent` from point-in-time event reads, then later enqueues based on the stale `created.deployment.status` object (not reloaded). With concurrent idempotent replays, one request can mark the deployment `failed` during precheck, while another request (that already passed the earlier checks) still appends `deployment_provider_precheck_passed`, enqueues, and returns success. This violates the intended “precheck gate before enqueue” behavior and can trigger queue work for a terminal failed deployment.  
  **Why blocking:** it reintroduces nondeterministic replay behavior under concurrency—the exact class of bug this fix is meant to close.
